project.key_breakthrough: diameter MAPE improved from 9% to 2.16% by switching to detection + blended circle formula (min(w,h)+sqrt(w*h))/2.
lessons_learned: Blended radius formula outperforms either min(w,h)/2 or sqrt(w*h)/2 alone — 2.16% vs 3.88% MAPE.
Expanding to no-catheter data (large 2-2.5um bubbles) was essential — model failed on unseen bubble sizes.
Detection + post-processing beats segmentation: avoids rectangular mask artifacts entirely.
dead_ends: YOLO segmentation masks — upsampled to 160x160 prototypes, inherently rectangular for circular objects.
sqrt(w*h)/2 alone overestimates ring-shaped large bubbles by ~20%.
min(w,h)/2 alone underestimates small bubbles — bias swings the wrong way.
project.trajectory: Started with segmentation at ~9% MAPE. Switched to detection + circle post-processing, expanded data to 30 images, and refined radius formula over 9 runs to reach 2.16% MAPE.
